introspection.description: GraphQL introspection query — enumerate entire schema

probes.description: Full introspection query to enumerate schema types and fields
Targeted introspection of Query root type
GET-based introspection bypass: GET /graphql?query={__schema{types{name}}} — bypasses POST-only introspection blocks

probes.payload: {"query":"{a:__typename b:__typename c:__typename d:__typename e:__typename f:__typename g:__typename h:__typename i:__typename j:__typename}"}
probes.description: Alias-based batch query (10 aliases in single request)

field_suggestion.description: GraphQL field suggestion exploitation — leak schema via typos

probes.description: Misspelled field to trigger 'Did you mean' suggestions
SQL injection via GraphQL resolver argument

mutation_injection.description: Injection via GraphQL mutations — SQLi/XSS in mutation arguments

probes.description: SQLi in mutation argument — look for SQL error in response
XSS via mutation argument — check if payload reflected in response

directive_overloading.description: GraphQL directive overloading — DoS via many directives on single field

probes.description: Abuse @skip/@include directives with many unknown directives — may trigger 500 or severe slowdown

persisted_query_bypass.description: Automatic Persisted Query (APQ) bypass — access cached operations via hash

probes.description: APQ probe — server may reveal supported operations or error with PersistedQuery info

batch_array.description: GraphQL batch query via JSON array — bypasses per-operation rate limiting

probes.description: Array batch with 5 queries — check if all execute (response is array of 5 results)

subscription_discovery.description: GraphQL subscription endpoint discovery — often has weaker auth than query endpoints

probes.description: WebSocket subscription handshake — check if subscription endpoint accepts connections

alias_amplification.description: Query complexity attack via aliases — one request, many operations. Critical use case: bypass rate-limit on auth/login.

probes.payload: {"query":"{a1:__typename a2:__typename a3:__typename a4:__typename a5:__typename a6:__typename a7:__typename a8:__typename a9:__typename a10:__typename a11:__typename a12:__typename a13:__typename a14:__typename a15:__typename a16:__typename a17:__typename a18:__typename a19:__typename a20:__typename}"}
probes.description: 20 aliased operations in single query — test query depth/complexity limits
Aliased login — 5 password attempts in one HTTP request bypasses rate-limit (1 HTTP request = 1 attempt). Scale to 100+ for credential stuffing.

persisted_query_hash_attacks.description: Persisted query / APQ attacks — hash-query mismatch, anonymous replay, hash collision.

probes.description: Hash-query mismatch — server SHOULD reject if sha256(query) != hash. Some libs trust the query when hash is wrong, allowing arbitrary mutation under whitelisted hash.
Replay known admin-query hash without auth headers. Some APQ caches don't tie hashes to user/role — admin queries become anonymous-callable.

csrf_via_get.description: GraphQL over GET — server accepts queries via GET parameters, opening CSRF surface for state-changing mutations.

probes.description: Issue mutation via GET. If server processes, CSRF is trivial via

deep_recursion.description: Schema cycle exploitation — recursively self-referential types lead to exponential expansion.

probes.description: 10-level deep recursion. If accepted (no max-depth limit) and timing >2x baseline, DoS confirmed.

federation_service_exposure.description: Apollo Federation gateways expose `_service` and `_entities` fields. `_service.sdl` returns the FULL federated schema (often with internal subgraph types), bypassing introspection-disabled gateways. `_entities` allows cross-subgraph entity resolution which often skips per-subgraph auth.

probes.description: Apollo Federation _service.sdl — leaks full federated schema even when introspection is disabled. SDL contains internal types, directives, and subgraph composition.
_entities cross-subgraph IDOR — gateway resolves entity by typename+key without per-subgraph auth check. Common bug: User subgraph trusts gateway-asserted ID without verifying caller.
Combined _service + _entities probe — distinguishes Apollo Federation from monolithic GraphQL.
@requires / @provides directive bypass — gateway-only directives that subgraphs may not enforce. internalRole may be exposed to caller without proper auth.

union_type_inline_fragment_leak.description: GraphQL union types (e.g. SearchResult = User | Post | Document). Inline fragments on union let attacker request fields from types they shouldn't see (admin-only, deleted documents).

probes.description: Union inline fragment fetches admin-only or soft-deleted content. If returned in any branch, type-level auth is missing.

typo_field_suggestion.description: GraphQL field-suggestion mode (Apollo/graphql-js) — typo in query reveals correct field via 'Did you mean' error. Information disclosure.

probes.description: Mistype field name → server suggests correct name. Confirms suggestion mode enabled (often misconfigured in prod).

partial_introspection.description: Partial introspection probe — even when full {__schema{...}} is blocked, single-field probes (queryType) may slip through WAF.

probes.description: 1-field introspection — bypasses introspection-disabled WAF rule if rule matches only deep queries

subscription_protocol_drift_2025.description: GraphQL subscription endpoints commonly support BOTH graphql-ws (modern) and subscriptions-transport-ws (legacy) protocols. Auth gating frequently differs between protocols — legacy protocol may skip auth/origin/CSRF checks the modern protocol enforces. 2025 H2 OWASP API Security research.

probes.description: Send WebSocket upgrade with Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: graphql-transport-ws (modern) vs subscriptions-transport-ws (legacy). Compare accepted protocols + whether auth differs. Active in W18.

subscription_auth_skip_legacy_protocol.description: Subscribe to a privileged stream (e.g. !subscription { adminEvents { ... } }) over the legacy protocol. If accepted unauth where modern protocol rejects, auth gating drift confirmed.

probes.description: Send privileged subscription over legacy protocol; modern protocol would 401, legacy may accept.

apollo_federation_field_authz_drift_2025.description: Apollo Federation field-level authz drift — entity resolution authorised but per-field guards on the resolved type missed by subgraph. Distinct from federation_service_exposure (which covers entity-level IDOR via _entities): this is FIELD-LEVEL — same victim entity is fetched legitimately, but @requires/@inaccessible fields on the type are leaked through. 2025 wave: subgraphs assume gateway enforces field-level @inaccessible / @authenticated directives, gateway assumes subgraph does.

probes.payload: {"query":"query($r:[_Any!]!){ _entities(representations:$r){ ...on User { id email internalRole apiKey lastIpAddress paymentMethodDigits }}}","variables":{"r":[{"__typename":"User","id":"<self_id>"}]}}
probes.description: Fetch SELF entity via _entities and request fields normally hidden by gateway @inaccessible directives (internalRole / apiKey / lastIpAddress / paymentMethodDigits). Subgraph that resolves directly leaks these because @inaccessible is gateway-only metadata. Use self ID first to avoid Rule 7 / Rule 8 — proves the field exposure without touching another user.
Account entity — operator-internal fields. Same pattern: subgraph resolves type directly, returning admin-only fields the gateway would have filtered via @inaccessible / @authenticated.

graphql_mutation_aliasing_account_recove.description: HackerOne disclosed pattern (high-frequency 2025-2026 H1) — single GraphQL request issues N aliased mutations against a rate-limited recovery endpoint (forgot-password, MFA-resend, phone-verification). Server processes each alias as a distinct call but the per-IP rate limiter sees ONE request; effectively bypasses the limiter by a factor of N. Used for SMS/email spam-bombing, recovery-code exhaustion, account-DoS. Distinct from generic rate-limit absence — this is rate-limit BYPASS via the GraphQL query layer.

probes.payload: {"query": "mutation { a1: sendRecoveryCode(email: \"victim@x.tld\") b1: sendRecoveryCode(email: \"victim@x.tld\") c1: sendRecoveryCode(email: \"victim@x.tld\") d1: sendRecoveryCode(email: \"victim@x.tld\") e1: sendRecoveryCode(email: \"victim@x.tld\") }"}
probes.description: 5 aliased mutations to sendRecoveryCode in one request. Vulnerable server processes all 5; rate-limiter sees ONE request. Confirm out-of-band (count SMS/email delivered to operator-owned victim) OR via aliased-field reflection in JSON response.

probes.payload: {"query": "mutation { a: verifyOtp(code: \"000000\") b: verifyOtp(code: \"111111\") c: verifyOtp(code: \"222222\") d: verifyOtp(code: \"333333\") e: verifyOtp(code: \"444444\") }"}
probes.description: Brute-force aliased OTP attempts. Rate-limit bypass + brute-force in one request. Any success-shape (token/verified:true) in any alias = CRITICAL: OTP brute-forceable.
